prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `9ba4a9aa`.
codex_response:
Implemented the ARC2 package for `9ba4a9aa`. The rule is: find the unique 3x3 checkerboard marker, take the single non-background cell touching it, follow that same-color dotted component using step-2 orthogonal connectivity, and extract the 3x3 ring block that component touches.

I used the `arc2_opus46_summary.json` hint only as a partial starting point and discarded the `arc2_sonnet45_summary.jsonl` heuristic. The sonnet summary’s “bottom-right quadrant” selection rule is wrong; the actual selector is the checker-adjacent same-color dotted component. The generator matches the family by building fixed-size 22x22 sparse dotted-tree layouts with one checkerboard start, one target ring on the main component, and several distractor rings on differently colored side branches. No new primitive was added to [`arc2/dsl_ext.py`](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/dsl_ext.py).
